strengths.evidence: Elite ultra-endurance capacity: 428km single rides, 8-stage races, multi-day efforts
Very strong aerobic base: avg ride HR 116-120 bpm at moderate power

limiters.evidence: Climbing deficit: training in flat Estonia, TBR demands 24,500m
Current volume (~200km/week outdoor) well below peak capacity

constraints: Flat terrain only (Estonia) — must simulate climbing indoors
8 weeks to race — specificity/sharpening phase, not base building

phases.name: Peak
phases.focus: Race-specific simulation, highest volume. Week 6 ends Sat May 16 before forced break.

phases.name: Forced Break
phases.focus: No training May 17-23. Acts as extended taper.

phases.name: Race Week
phases.focus: Shakeout rides, travel to Trieste, race start May 29

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery. Yoga or stretching optional.

workouts.name: Indoor Climbing Intervals
workouts.description: Simulate sustained climbing on trainer. Use ERG mode or gradient simulation.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 4 x 10min at 5-7% gradient (Z3, 180-213W) at 65-75rpm, 5min Z1 recovery
Cool-down: 15min Z1

Low cadence to simulate real climbing. Focus on smooth pedaling under load.

workouts.name: Easy Endurance Ride
workouts.description: Outdoor or trainer. Steady Zone 2. Practice fueling at 60g carbs/hour.
workouts.humanReadable:
90min steady Z2 (133-178W, HR 126-138)
Fueling practice: take on 60g carbs/hr
Keep cadence 85-95rpm

workouts.name: Strength Training
workouts.description: Focus on posterior chain and core for bikepacking durability
workouts.humanReadable:
Circuit (3 rounds):
- Squats: 3x8
- Romanian deadlift: 3x8
- Step-ups: 3x10 each leg
- Plank: 3x60s
- Dead bug: 3x12 each side
- Back extensions: 3x12

workouts.name: Sweet Spot Climbing
workouts.description: Indoor sweet spot work at low cadence to build climbing power
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 3 x 15min at 88-93% FTP (208-220W) at 65-70rpm, 5min Z1 recovery
Cool-down: 10min Z1

workouts.name: Easy Recovery Spin
workouts.description: Very easy spin or rest day. Listen to body.
workouts.humanReadable:
45min Z1 (<130W, HR <126)
Very easy, coffee ride pace
Optional: substitute with yoga/stretching

workouts.name: Long Endurance Ride
workouts.description: Outdoor long ride. Build toward race-day duration. Practice nutrition.
workouts.humanReadable:
4h outdoor ride, ~100km
Z2 effort (HR 126-138)
Practice race nutrition: 60-80g carbs/hr
Include any available hills
Carry bikepacking-style load if possible

workouts.name: Back-to-Back Ride (Day 2)
workouts.description: Ride on fatigued legs from yesterday. Simulates multi-day racing.
workouts.humanReadable:
2.5h outdoor ride on tired legs
Z2 effort, accept slightly lower power than usual
Fueling practice continues
Note how legs feel at start — this is how TBR Day 2+ will feel

weeks.focus: Increase volume, longer climbing blocks, introduce intensity

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery. Foam roll, stretching.

workouts.name: Extended Climbing Intervals
workouts.description: Longer climbing blocks to build sustained climbing endurance
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 3 x 15min at 5-8% gradient (Z3, 180-213W) at 65-70rpm, 5min Z1 recovery
Finish: 1 x 10min at Z4 (216-235W) — threshold climbing
Cool-down: 10min Z1

workouts.name: Steady Endurance
workouts.description: Zone 2 endurance with nutrition practice
workouts.humanReadable:
1h45 steady Z2 (133-178W)
Practice eating real food on the bike (bars, rice cakes, sandwiches)
Target 70g carbs/hr

workouts.name: Strength Training
workouts.description: Lower body and core focus
workouts.humanReadable:
Circuit (3 rounds):
- Front squats: 3x8
- Single-leg deadlift: 3x8 each
- Bulgarian split squat: 3x8 each
- Pallof press: 3x10 each side
- Hip thrusts: 3x12
- Side plank: 3x40s each

workouts.name: Over-Under Climbing
workouts.description: Over-under intervals simulate surging on climbs — push over threshold, settle below
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2 with 3x30s accelerations
Main: 4 x 8min [(2min @ 95% FTP/225W, 1min @ 108% FTP/256W) x2 + 2min @ 95%], 5min Z1 recovery
Cool-down: 15min Z1

Low cadence (65-70rpm) throughout intervals

workouts.name: Active Recovery
workouts.description: Yoga, stretching, or complete rest. Prepare for weekend block.
workouts.humanReadable:
Yoga/stretching session
Focus on hip flexors, lower back, neck, shoulders
Bike fit areas that get tight during long rides

workouts.name: Long Ride with Tempo Blocks
workouts.description: Extended outdoor ride with tempo effort blocks to build muscular endurance
workouts.humanReadable:
5h outdoor ride, ~120km
First 2h: Z2 steady
Hours 3-4: Include 3 x 15min Z3 tempo (180-213W) with 5min Z2 between
Final hour: Z2 easy

Practice race nutrition: 70-80g carbs/hr
Carry bikepacking bags if available

workouts.name: Back-to-Back Long Ride (Day 2)
workouts.description: Multi-day simulation. Ride on tired legs.
workouts.humanReadable:
3.5h outdoor ride on fatigued legs
All Z2, no intensity
Practice starting when legs feel heavy — this IS race prep
Continue nutrition practice

weeks.focus: Peak build week — highest pre-recovery volume, extended climbing

workouts.name: Recovery Spin
workouts.description: Easy spin to flush legs after weekend block
workouts.humanReadable:
45min Z1 easy spin
High cadence (95+rpm), very low power
No effort, just blood flow

workouts.name: Long Climbing Simulation
workouts.description: Extended indoor climbing to simulate TBR mountain stages. Key session.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 2h continuous climbing simulation
- Alternate 10min Z3 (180-213W) at 65rpm with 10min Z2 (133-178W) at 75rpm
- Simulate gradient changes between 4-10%
Cool-down: 15min Z1

This simulates the sustained climbing of a TBR mountain day
Fuel: 70g carbs/hr throughout

workouts.name: Moderate Endurance
workouts.description: Outdoor endurance with bikepacking setup practice
workouts.humanReadable:
2h outdoor ride with full bikepacking bags
Z2 effort (the bags will make this harder)
Practice bike handling with loaded bike
Note any comfort issues for bike fit adjustment

workouts.name: Strength — Endurance Focus
workouts.description: Higher reps, moderate weight — shifting toward maintenance
workouts.humanReadable:
Circuit (2-3 rounds):
- Goblet squats: 3x12
- Single-leg calf raises: 3x15 each
- Prone Y-T-W raises: 3x10 (shoulder stability for aero position)
- Core circuit: plank 60s, side plank 30s each, bird dog 10 each

workouts.name: Sustained Tempo
workouts.description: Long tempo blocks at race-realistic effort — this is your race pace on climbs
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 2 x 25min Z3 tempo (180-213W) at 65-75rpm, 10min Z2 between
Finish: 1 x 5min Z4 (216-235W) — end-of-climb push
Cool-down: 10min Z1

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Complete rest before big weekend. Pack nutrition, check bike.

workouts.name: Epic Long Ride
workouts.description: Longest ride of the build phase. Full bikepacking rehearsal.
workouts.humanReadable:
7h outdoor ride, ~165km
Z2 effort throughout
Full race nutrition rehearsal: 70-80g carbs/hr (bars, gels, real food mix)
Ride with loaded bikepacking bags
Practice cafe/shop resupply stops

This is your Solstice 100-miles effort level — you know how to do this

workouts.name: Back-to-Back Long Ride (Day 2)
workouts.description: Extended day 2 ride on tired legs. Key race simulation.
workouts.humanReadable:
4h outdoor ride on fatigued legs
Z2, accept reduced power output
This mirrors TBR Day 2-3 fatigue
Continue nutrition practice
Note: if legs feel destroyed, reduce to 3h — recovery week is next

weeks.focus: Absorb 3-week training load. Easy riding only. Mental refresh.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Complete rest.

workouts.name: Easy Spin
workouts.description: Very easy recovery ride
workouts.humanReadable:
60min Z1-Z2 easy spin
No structure, just enjoy the bike
Outdoor preferred for mental refresh

workouts.name: Yoga / Stretching
workouts.description: Active recovery focusing on hip mobility and back
workouts.humanReadable:
45min yoga session
Focus: hip flexors, hamstrings, lower back, neck/shoulders
Gentle — this is recovery, not a workout

workouts.name: Easy Ride with Openers
workouts.description: Easy ride with a few short accelerations to keep legs sharp
workouts.humanReadable:
60min mostly Z1-Z2
Include 4 x 30s accelerations to Z4-Z5 with 3min easy between
These 'openers' maintain neuromuscular engagement without adding fatigue

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Complete rest. Prepare gear for peak phase.

workouts.name: Moderate Endurance Ride
workouts.description: Ease back into riding with a moderate outdoor ride
workouts.humanReadable:
2.5h outdoor ride, Z2
Should feel easy and refreshing
If legs still feel heavy, cut to 2h

workouts.name: Easy Ride
workouts.description: Short easy ride to confirm legs are recovered
workouts.humanReadable:
75min Z2
If legs feel snappy and fresh, you're ready for the peak block
If still tired, take Monday as full rest too

weeks.focus: Race-specific simulation. Highest volume week. Multi-day block.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Complete rest before biggest week of the plan

workouts.name: Marathon Climbing Session
workouts.description: The key indoor climbing session. Simulates a full TBR mountain day.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 2.5h continuous climbing simulation
- Alternate 15min Z3 (180-213W) at 60-70rpm with 10min Z2 (133-178W) at 75rpm
- Simulate a virtual climbing route with sustained gradients
- Total climbing time: ~90min at Z3
Cool-down: 10min Z1

Fuel: 80g carbs/hr — this is race fueling rate
Mental: Practice settling into discomfort. This IS the race.

workouts.name: Endurance with Loaded Bike
workouts.description: Outdoor ride with full bikepacking setup
workouts.humanReadable:
2.5h outdoor with bikepacking bags loaded
All Z2 — practice bike handling, comfort
Test any new equipment or clothing for the race
Note: reduce intensity if Tuesday's session left you fatigued

workouts.name: Strength — Maintenance
workouts.description: Reduced strength work. Last proper strength session before the race.
workouts.humanReadable:
Maintenance only (2 rounds):
- Squats: 2x8 (lighter weight)
- Single-leg deadlift: 2x8 each
- Core circuit: plank 45s, dead bug 10 each

This is the last strength session before TBR

workouts.name: Tempo Climbing Blocks
workouts.description: Race-pace climbing with sustained tempo efforts
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 2 x 20min Z3 at 65rpm (simulated 6-8% grade, 180-213W), 8min Z2 between
Finish: 1 x 10min descending at high cadence (100+rpm, Z2)
Cool-down: 10min Z1

Practice descending position too — TBR has long descents

workouts.name: Easy Spin + Prep
workouts.description: Very easy ride. Prepare gear and nutrition for weekend block.
workouts.humanReadable:
45min Z1 easy spin
Prepare weekend gear:
- Pack nutrition for 2 days
- Check bike mechanics
- Plan routes for Saturday and Sunday

workouts.name: Peak Long Ride — Race Simulation
workouts.description: The biggest ride of the plan. Full race rehearsal day.
workouts.humanReadable:
8h outdoor ride, ~180km
Z2 with natural terrain variation
Full race nutrition: 80g carbs/hr for the entire ride
Bikepacking bags loaded

This is your final 'proof of concept' ride.
Aim to finish strong, not fast.
Practice:
- Eating every 30min
- Hydration (target 500-750ml/hr)
- Saddle comfort over 6+ hours
- Navigation (use GPS as you will in the race)

workouts.name: Back-to-Back Day 2 — Fatigue Riding
workouts.description: Ride on deeply fatigued legs. The most race-specific session of the plan.
workouts.humanReadable:
5h on tired legs after yesterday's 8h ride
Z2 effort, accept lower power

This 13h weekend block (8h + 5h) simulates consecutive TBR days.
Pay attention to:
- How long does it take for legs to loosen up?
- Any pain points (saddle, hands, neck)?
- Does nutrition strategy hold up on Day 2?
- Mental state — boredom, motivation

weeks.focus: Final training week. Last hard sessions Mon-Sat. Sunday May 17 starts forced break.

workouts.name: Recovery Spin
workouts.description: Easy recovery after peak weekend block
workouts.humanReadable:
45min Z1 easy spin
Flush legs after the big weekend
Gentle stretching afterward

workouts.name: Final Climbing Session
workouts.description: Last serious climbing-specific indoor session of the plan
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15min Z2
Main: 80min of climbing simulation
- 4 x 15min Z3 (180-213W) at 65rpm with 5min Z2 between
Cool-down: 10min Z1

Last chance to train climbing power. After this week, volume drops.

workouts.name: Endurance Ride — Final Equipment Shakedown
workouts.description: Outdoor endurance with exact race setup
workouts.humanReadable:
2h outdoor Z2
Ride with EXACT race setup — bags, clothing, lights, navigation
This is the final equipment check. Fix anything that doesn't work.
After today, no new gear.

workouts.name: Night Riding Session
workouts.description: Practice riding in darkness. TBR involves riding through the night.
workouts.humanReadable:
2h ride starting at dusk, finishing in full darkness
Z2 effort
Practice:
- Light setup (helmet + bar light)
- Navigation in darkness
- Managing drowsiness
- Eating/drinking in the dark

Start around 20:00-21:00 for May Estonian conditions

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Rest before final long ride

workouts.name: Final Long Ride — Confidence Builder
workouts.description: Last long ride before the break. Confidence builder, not fitness builder.
workouts.humanReadable:
5h outdoor ride, ~120km
Z2 throughout — no heroics
Full race setup: bike bags, nutrition plan, clothing

This ride should feel EASY. You've done way harder.
Goal: confirm everything works, enjoy the ride, build confidence.
Last hard day before 12-day taper+break into race start.

workouts.name: Break Starts
workouts.description: Unavailable. Forced break begins. The fitness is banked — trust it.

weeks.focus: No training. This serves as an extended taper. Deep recovery before TBR.

workouts.name: Break — Day 2
workouts.description: Rest. You have massive fitness banked from years of ultra-cycling and 6 weeks of focused TBR prep.

workouts.name: Break — Day 3
workouts.description: Rest. A 7-day break after a training block is a legitimate taper strategy for ultra events.

workouts.name: Break — Day 4
workouts.description: Rest. Stay active with walking, gentle stretching. Avoid sitting all day.

workouts.name: Break — Day 5
workouts.description: Rest. Begin thinking about race logistics: packing lists, nutrition plan, sleep strategy.

workouts.name: Break — Day 6
workouts.description: Rest. Review TBR route, checkpoints, cutoff times. Visualize the race.

workouts.name: Break — Day 7 (Last day off)
workouts.description: Final rest day. Tomorrow you return to the bike for shakeout rides.

workouts.name: Return-to-Bike Shakeout
workouts.description: First ride back after 7 days off. Easy spin with openers to wake up the legs.
workouts.humanReadable:
60min mostly Z1-Z2
First 30min: very easy, let the body remember what cycling feels like
Then: 5 x 30s at Z5 (237-249W) with 3min easy between

Legs may feel sluggish at first — totally normal after a week off.
The openers will wake up neuromuscular pathways.
Don't panic if power feels low — it's a taper effect, not fitness loss.

weeks.focus: Final shakeout rides, carb loading, travel to Trieste May 27-28. Race starts May 29 at 09:00.

workouts.name: Easy Ride with Openers
workouts.description: Short easy ride with a few race-pace efforts to confirm sharpness
workouts.humanReadable:
60min easy ride
Include 3 x 2min at Z3 (180-213W) with 3min Z1 between

Legs should feel significantly better than yesterday.
This confirms the taper is working.

Begin carb loading today: increase carb intake to 8-10g/kg/day

workouts.name: Final Pre-Travel Ride
workouts.description: Last ride before travel. Quick spin and final bike check.
workouts.humanReadable:
30min Z1 easy spin
2 x 15s acceleration to race start pace

Final bike check:
- Tire pressure / tubeless sealant fresh
- Chain lubed
- Lights charged + spare batteries packed
- GPS loaded with TBR route
- Bags packed and balanced
- Spare tube, patches, multi-tool, chain link

Pack bike for travel

workouts.name: Travel Day 1 — To Trieste
workouts.description: Travel to Trieste. Continue carb loading. Stay hydrated. Walk to keep blood flowing.

workouts.name: Pre-Race Day Spin in Trieste
workouts.description: Short shakeout spin around Trieste to confirm bike works after travel
workouts.humanReadable:
20min very easy spin around Trieste
Confirm bike is assembled correctly after travel:
- Shifters, brakes, lights, GPS all working
- Bags seated properly
- Nothing rattles

Evening: early dinner, high carb (pasta, rice)
Bed by 21:00-22:00 — race start is 09:00 Friday May 29

bike.notes: Ultra pacing: if you feel good, you're going too hard. Save matches for Day 5+.

dayByDay.day1: Conservative start. Resist the adrenaline. You know from Hydra — Day 1 pace sets the tone.
dayByDay.day2_3: Settle into rhythm. 14-16h riding days. Steady Z2 effort.
dayByDay.day4_5: The hard middle. Fatigue accumulates. This is where discipline matters most.
dayByDay.day6_7: You've done this before (Hydra E6-E7). Dig deep but stay smart.
dayByDay.day8_9: Smell the finish. Keep eating, keep pedaling. Don't take unnecessary risks on descents.

nutrition.preRace: Morning of May 29: 3h before start — 100-150g carbs (oatmeal, banana, toast). Coffee.

during.strategy: Eat before hungry. Drink before thirsty. Set a 30-minute alarm if needed.
during.products: Mix of gels, bars, and real food. Variety prevents flavor fatigue over 9 days.
during.dailyTarget: 6000-8000 kcal intake target (you'll burn more, but gut limits absorption)

nutrition.resupply: Plan resupply stops every 60-100km. Gas stations, bakeries, shops in towns along route.
nutrition.notes: Day 3-4 appetite may crash — force yourself to eat. Savory foods often work better than sweet after Day 2.

sleep.strategy: 4-5h per night, ideally 00:30-06:30 CEST (per your race simulation model)
sleep.notes: Bivvy/sleep setup must be fast: target 15min from stopping to sleeping. Pre-plan sleep spots on route. Earplugs + eye mask essential.

criticalChecklist: Lights: helmet + bar mount, fully charged, spare batteries or USB charging
Navigation: GPS with full route loaded, backup phone with offline maps
Repair: spare tube/patches, multi-tool, chain link, tire boot
Sleep: bivvy bag or ultralight tent, sleeping bag/quilt, inflatable pillow
Clothing: rain jacket, arm/leg warmers, wind vest (Balkan weather is variable)
Nutrition: frame bag + handlebar bag for food storage, water bottles/bladder

taper.notes: 12-day forced taper (May 17-28). Longer than ideal, but your deep ultra-endurance foundation means fitness loss is minimal over 12 days. The shakeout rides on May 24-26 will restore neuromuscular sharpness. Trust the process.
